notes: TSE founded, initially trading only corporate and government bonds
First-year trading value; equities were added later in the 1970s alongside bonds

Iran's rapid 1970s economic expansion + government policy of granting shares to employees of large state-owned/family-owned enterprises drove demand for equity trading, expanding TSE beyond its original bonds-only scope
Pre-revolution peak trading value -- a 2,280x increase over the 1967 opening-year figure (15 million to 34.2 billion rials)

Operations halted after the Islamic Revolution due to nationalization of major industries and a prohibition against interest-based financial activities under the new government; further suspended through the 1980-1988 Iran-Iraq War
TSE reopened via a Budget Act provision, restarting organized equity trading after roughly a decade of dormancy
TEPIX (Tehran Price Index, the All-Shares Price Index) introduced as the main market barometer -- a weighted market value of all listed share prices, updated every two minutes
'Within eight years [of the 1988 reopening] the exchange saw its numbers rise to 249 companies listed'
'Fewer than 5% of Iranians owned stock' as of 2005 -- illustrates how thin retail participation was before the 2009-2014 boom

Note: company count fell from 419 (2005) to 324 (2007) despite rising market cap -- likely reflects delistings/consolidations rather than market contraction; recorded as-is from source without reconciling
